meta.name: Citrus-Marinated Fish
meta.subtitle: Bright citrus + garlic + chili, cooked quickly (pan, grill, or oven) — not a long acid cure

summary: Fast, flavor-forward fish with a citrus-garlic-chili marinade that’s designed for short contact time (to avoid “cooking” the fish in acid). Great for tacos, bowls, or plated meals.

yield.notes: Plan 4–6 oz fish per person depending on sides.

optional.label: Grill
optional.notes: Great for firm fish; use a grill basket if needed.

optional.label: Parchment paper
optional.notes: Makes cleanup easier for oven method.

ingredients.label: Firm white fish fillets (cod, mahi-mahi, snapper, halibut)
notes: Aim for firm fish that holds up to quick searing.
If using delicate fish (tilapia/sole), prefer oven method and handle gently.

ingredients.label: Fresh lime juice
ingredients.notes: Short marinade only — do not exceed recommended time.

ingredients.label: Fresh orange juice
ingredients.notes: Adds sweetness and rounds lime sharpness.

ingredients.label: Chili powder
ingredients.notes: Use mild chili powder for low heat; ancho is excellent.

steps.title: Make the citrus-chili marinade
instructions: In a bowl, whisk together lime juice, orange juice, lime zest, garlic, olive oil, chili powder, cumin, salt, pepper, and honey (if using).
Set aside 2 tablespoons of the marinade for serving (do not contaminate it with raw fish).
checks: Marinade tastes bright, slightly salty, and gently spicy
Reserved portion is kept separate from raw fish

steps.title: Marinate briefly (important for texture)
instructions: Pat fish dry. Add fish to the marinade and turn gently to coat.
Refrigerate 10–15 minutes for firm fish. Do not exceed 20 minutes (acid can start changing texture).

steps.title: Choose your cook method
instructions: Skillet (fastest): Heat a nonstick or well-seasoned skillet over medium-high until hot.
Oven: Preheat to 425°F / 218°C. Line a sheet pan with parchment if desired.
Grill: Preheat to medium-high. Oil grates and use a grill basket for delicate fillets.

steps.title: Cook the fish
instructions: Skillet: Remove fish from marinade (let excess drip off). Sear 2–4 minutes per side depending on thickness until opaque and flakes easily.
Oven: Arrange fish on sheet pan. Roast 8–12 minutes until opaque and flaky (time depends on thickness).
Grill: Grill 2–4 minutes per side for firm fish; avoid moving too early.
checks: Fish is opaque and flakes easily with a fork
Surface has light browning without burnt garlic

steps.title: Finish and serve
instructions: Drizzle reserved marinade over cooked fish (or serve on the side).
Top with cilantro (optional) and serve with lime wedges.
Great in tacos, bowls, or on plates with rice and vegetables.

refrigerator.notes: Fish is best day-of. Store cooked leftovers tightly covered.

reheat.notes: Avoid microwaving on high; it can toughen fish. Add reserved sauce after reheating.

handling: Do not reuse marinade that contacted raw fish unless boiled for at least 1 minute.

internalTemp.notes: 145°F / 63°C is commonly referenced as a safe cooked fish temp.

variations.label: Lemon-lime version
notes: Swap half the orange juice for lemon juice for sharper citrus.
Add 1 tsp grated ginger for extra brightness.

variations.label: Spicier version
notes: Add 1/4 tsp cayenne or 1 minced jalapeño.
Finish with chili flakes and extra lime zest.

variations.label: Taco-style fish
notes: Dust fish lightly with extra chili powder before searing for deeper color.
Serve with slaw and a squeeze of lime; add a creamy drizzle if desired.

weeknightNotes: Marinate fish only 10–15 minutes while you prep sides.
Use oven method if cooking multiple fillets at once.
